title: PIE Weekly Bridge Playbook
description: Stand up a weekly PIE (Problems, Incidents, Escalations) bridge between engineering and support leadership: shared hour, live scenarios, prioritization, relationship repair, and detection of escalation abuse from sales or other groups.

mindset.principle: If everything is escalated, nothing is. A standing room with eng and support kills the punt culture faster than another ticket field.

mistakes.label: Throw work over the wall then ding support for the outcome
mistakes.cost: Support hated; engineers avoid; customers wait

mistakes.label: Engineers refuse basics with no training path for support
Sales escalates to cover bad sale, fit miss, or silence
mistakes.cost: Eng and support fight each other while root cause sits in sales

mistakes.label: Only ad-hoc war rooms when on fire
mistakes.cost: No relationship equity when the fire starts

pie_blocks.label: Open problems both teams are working (not only tickets)
Live or recent scenarios: what worked, what failed
Deep dive and prioritize the escalation queue

decision_branches.situation: Support is the dump zone; eng and support hate each other
Escalations trace to sales/SE covering their own issues
Engineers tired of the same basic questions

steps.name: Charter PIE with eng + support leads
steps.goal: One hour weekly, both rooms equal

steps.name: Tag escalation source
steps.goal: Real tech vs sales cover vs training gap

agent_signal.use_when: Support and engineering in conflict. Escalations everywhere. Sales punts. Need weekly PIE-style problems/incidents/escalations forum with shared ownership.
decision_criteria: Co-own the hour: eng director-level + support lead.
Define what may escalate; audit sources weekly.
Return basics to training/KB so eng is not the FAQ.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: smaller server/storage scale-up. PIE fixed relationship in weeks and exposed escalation abuse.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic job spine. Not a substitute for incident command tooling alone.

Need playbook: PIE Weekly Bridge Playbook
